title: The Agent identifies the Datadog reclass journal entry (entry_id je_1ce7247752034cbc, display id JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-05-0044, $28,400, DR 521000 / CR 500000, Acme Cloud, period acme_cloud_FP-2026-05) as the close-package item under review and summarizes the differing positions or explanations discovered during the investigation.
justification: Matthew's request is centered on understanding what the contested item is and whether conflicting interpretations exist before a partner disposition is made.
evidence: Final response identifies the Datadog reclass JE as the item under review and surfaces the competing explanations (e.g., the JE-narrative one-time category correction view vs. the ~$340K cumulative FY25 misclassification view) from emails, Slack, the JE narrative, and supporting documentation.

title: The Agent reaches a clear conclusion in the final response on whether the current accounting treatment of the Datadog reclass is supportable, and grounds that conclusion in the underlying evidence reviewed.
justification: Matthew explicitly prioritizes defensibility and audit quality over the dollar amount involved, so the disposition must be an explicit supportability conclusion tied to evidence.
evidence: Final response includes a clear supportable / not-supportable conclusion on the treatment and references specific supporting evidence (e.g., JE detail, SAB 99 memo state, AP source data, account history, prior-period precedent).

title: The Agent identifies any missing approvals, documentation gaps, unresolved review comments, or outstanding follow-up items associated with the Datadog reclass and the surrounding Acme Cloud FP-2026-05 close package.
justification: Unresolved items could prevent partner sign-off and must be surfaced before a final decision is made.
evidence: Final response lists outstanding deficiencies (e.g., SAB 99 memo substance, missing JE attachments / AP-autoroute support, unresolved reviewer concerns, incomplete BlackLine reconciliations or close tasks) or explicitly confirms none remain.

title: The Agent compares the current Datadog reclass treatment against prior occurrences, related reviews, or historical treatment (e.g., the FP-2025-09 reclass precedent JE-acme_cloud-FP-2025-09-0041 ('Cloud infrastructure spend' reclass), prior-period Datadog coding to account 500000, or the April 2026 Helix Bio gross-margin context) and identifies any inconsistencies.
justification: The prompt requires determining whether the issue is isolated or part of a broader pattern, which requires explicit comparison against prior-period treatment.
evidence: Final response references one of the listed prior examples (the FP-2025-09 reclass precedent JE-acme_cloud-FP-2025-09-0041, prior-period Datadog GL coding to account 500000, or April 2026 Helix Bio onboarding context) and explains whether the current treatment is consistent or breaks comparability.

title: The Agent provides a clear partner-level recommendation on whether the matter is ready for sign-off, requires remediation before sign-off, or should be escalated, with the rationale supporting the recommendation.
justification: Matthew needs a decision-oriented recommendation rather than only investigative findings.
evidence: Final response contains a specific recommendation (sign / hold / remediate / escalate) and the rationale that supports it.

title: The Agent records the partner review conclusion in a formal review artifact tied to the Acme Cloud acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 close package by one of the following paths: uploading a new memo or working paper to the Records Vault (linked to the close package or to journal entry je_1ce7247752034cbc), adding a new version to the existing Final Management Accounts Package document (doc_fc23774ed7d84f3f, which carries the embedded Datadog SAB 99 memo), or adding a BlackLine review note on the related Acme Cloud reconciliation or close task.
justification: Matthew explicitly requires a documented audit trail that can support future review; an email alone is not a substitute for a review record in the documentation systems of record.
evidence: Trajectory shows a successful write call (records_vault_upload_document, records_vault_add_document_version on doc_fc23774ed7d84f3f, or blackline_create_review_note) tied to the Acme Cloud acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 Datadog reclass.

title: The Agent's review-record artifact (Records Vault memo, a new version of the existing Final Management Accounts Package doc_fc23774ed7d84f3f, or a BlackLine review note) includes the issue reviewed, the supporting evidence considered, the conclusion reached, and the rationale for the recommendation.
justification: A documentation update alone is insufficient unless it captures the reasoning that supports the conclusion so a future reviewer can defend it.
evidence: The content of the written artifact (document body, review-note text, or JE annotation) contains all four elements: issue, evidence, conclusion, and rationale.

title: The Agent communicates the review outcome to the directly involved stakeholders Andrea Phil (andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com), Jones Harrison (jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com), and Brenda Abbas (brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com) via email, Messaging, or Slack (DM or channel post).
justification: The relevant reviewers, preparer, and requesting partner must be informed of the final disposition so understanding is consistent across the close team.
evidence: Trajectory shows an outbound communication call (email_send_email, messaging_send_message, or slack_conversations_add_message) addressed to Andrea, Jones, and Brenda (as direct recipients or cc'd), with the partner review outcome in the body.

title: The Agent's stakeholder communication includes the issue reviewed, the key findings, the recommendation, and the required next steps.
justification: Stakeholders need the findings, recommendation, and next steps to understand and act on the disposition; a notification without these content elements is not actionable.
evidence: Communication content references the Datadog reclass, the partner-level findings, the recommendation (sign / hold / remediate / escalate), and the actions required before sign-off can proceed.

title: The Agent reports in the final response whether the Datadog reclass issue appears to be an isolated occurrence or evidence of a recurring process weakness (e.g., the Datadog AP auto-routing default coding to account 500000 across FY25).
justification: The prompt specifically asks whether the matter represents a broader governance or process concern rather than a one-off item.
evidence: Final response explicitly classifies the issue as isolated or recurring and provides supporting reasoning (e.g., references the AP vendor-coding default behavior, the ~$340K cumulative FY25 figure, or the absence of a root-cause control fix).

title: The Agent completes the review investigation and reaches a recommendation before sending any stakeholder communication of the final disposition.
justification: Stakeholder communications should be based on completed analysis rather than preliminary findings; ordering between the investigation and the outbound communication cannot be captured by any single Outcome rubric on the communication itself.
evidence: Trajectory shows the investigative read/search tool calls (Oracle GL, SAP Subledger, BlackLine, Records Vault, Email, Slack, Messaging) and the review conclusion forming before the outbound stakeholder communication call is made.
